statement: In real-cohesive cubical Agda, the effective object of both the Q-Free witness ladder and the located spectrum is one CohesiveCorridor extending ModalSplitCertificate: two walls = the shape/flat modal split, modulus = cohesion, cost = the flat wall, open gap implies finite computable rate, phi via goldenInflation, Part II = SequentialColimit AF, Part III = ZCohomology H1.
intent: Realize the steelmanned corridor thesis natively in the repo's real-cohesive cubical-Agda substrate, where anti-hack constraints become typing facts.

approaches.strategy: A1 modal-split corridor spine with Conway grounding and adjacent consumer repair
approaches.reflexion: A1 is accepted, but the full A1-A15+M1 conjecture remains open. The PM assumed Substrate.LawsOfForm and Substrate.Eigenform existed and that agda_boundary_compile handled repo-qualified modules. Both assumptions were false on disk; repairing them was necessary before the corridor wrapper could be a verified construction.

approaches.strategy: A2 climbing ladder instance consuming A1 corridor
approaches.reflexion: A2 is accepted as a constructive Agda witness package: the ladder reuses A1 CohesiveCorridor instead of defining a parallel corridor; its BiHeyting proof now depends on logic-base, and its lower wall is tied both to the corridor projection and shapeFlatTransport. Full A1-A15+M1 conjecture remains open.

approaches.strategy: A3 located spectrum over SmoothWitnessLedger consuming A1/A2 corridor spine
approaches.reflexion: A3 accepted; locatedness/apartness/roundedness/nonconstancy/smooth-shape/qphase are projected from SmoothWitnessLedger, with connected non-vacuity facts. Full A1-A15+M1 conjecture remains open.

approaches.strategy: A4 Modulus = nucleus via ClosureTraceGlue and shapeFlatIso roundtrip
approaches.reflexion: Constructive content is on disk: A4 consumes A1-A3, uses ClosureTraceGlue certificate, avoids metrics/truncations/postulates, and registry checker validates free-compute authority. Next phase is A5 cost-is-flat-wall kill-switch.

approaches.strategy: A5 cost-is-flat-wall via modal split and shapeFlatIso witness
approaches.reflexion: Constructive content is on disk: FlatWallCost consumes A1-A4, exposes cost as the flat wall instead of an independent quantity, scopes shapeFlatIso compatibility to the corridor witness carrier, and A5 oracle rejects drop-shapeFlatIso, independent-cost, and drop-ledger-split variants. Next phase is A6 gap implies finite computable rate.

approaches.strategy: A6 gap implies finite computable rate via DedekindApart separator
approaches.reflexion: Constructive content is on disk: FiniteGapRate consumes A5 FlatWallCost, projects the located spectrum from the modulus instance, computes the Nat rate as rationalFiniteRate of the actual DedekindApart separator, consumes leftLower and rightNotLower, preserves located/rounded projections, and rejects constant-rate/ignored-separator/detached-ledger fixtures. Next phase is A7 phi self-similarity.

approaches.strategy: A7 Phi self-similar via golden Pisot matrix witness and finite-gap corridor spine
approaches.reflexion: Constructive BHK witness shape is SelfSimilarityWitness: U.T_UNIFY plus P.goldenInflation, P.goldenInflation-sq, and P.goldenInflation-not-idem; PhiSelfSimilarCorridor consumes A6 finite rate, A5 flat wall cost, and A4 closure idempotence without adding local phi, metric, postulate, norm, truncation, or runtime claim.

approaches.strategy: A8 Part II Z[phi] AF SequentialColimit via Fibonacci finite rungs over A7 phi-self-similar corridor
approaches.reflexion: Constructive witness is the Agda record ZPhiAFColimitCorridor: finite Fibonacci rungs, HIT-style SequentialColimit incl/push, imported A7 golden/Pisot generator square law and movement witness, and per-rung refinement projection back to the A7 corridor. Second pass added downstream projections for Bratteli arrow, colimit carrier, generator identity, and A7 rate/cost/stride reuse; oracle requires them and rejects bad fixtures.

approaches.strategy: A9 H1/logical-entropy screening consumes A8 SequentialColimit/Phi spine and exposes conditional mirror interface
approaches.reflexion: Accepted: A9 builds a constructive finite carry H1 screen over the A8 Z[phi] AF colimit. The screen is not a label-only H1 claim: it carries rank-one closed-not-boundary data, open-chain zero control, positive logical-entropy distinction count, A8 rate/cost/stride reuse, conditional mirror projections, hostile bad-fixture rejection, body-substitution, and cubical extraction receipts.

approaches.strategy: A10 aggregate oracle/report composes A1-A9 evidence and indexes Boundary artifacts
approaches.reflexion: Accepted: phase_a10_aggregate_oracle.py passes with 215 checks, 0 failures, all A1-A9 oracles replayed, 150 JSON artifacts indexed, A10 body_substitution and cubical_extraction receipts green; no runtime/metric authority added.

approaches.strategy: A11 Invert bridge: finite build dashv invert PIE witness over A8/A9 and Lafont/CDL evaluator receipts
approaches.reflexion: Accepted: InvertBridge.agda Boundary-compiles; phase_a11_oracle.py validates A8/A9 spine, forward/backward evaluator JSON, bracket/croissant/param-tie finite rows, no Boundary readback/runtime overclaim, and bad-fixture rejection; A11 body_substitution and cubical_extraction receipts green.

approaches.strategy: A12 Universal modulus (Lafont epsilon/delta/gamma V-suite)
approaches.reflexion: Accepted A12 as finite constructive corridor certificate: UniversalModulus.agda consumes A11 BuildInvertPIEBridge, carries epsilon/delta/gamma V-suite counts (6 fixtures/classes/readbacks, 12 strategy/kernel anchors, 48/48 mutants), preserves A5 flat-wall cost, and keeps corridor Boundary readback open. No metric or runtime overclaim introduced.

approaches.strategy: A13 Cayley-Dickson vertical lift (finite Cl21 structural loss profile)
approaches.reflexion: Accepted: A13 now consumes A12 UniversalModulusBridge and Substrate.Cl21 safe core; preserves A5 flat-wall via A12; binds finite Cl(2,1) signature 2/1/3 and S3 dimension; keeps Boundary scope at R7 artifact only with no lowerer/runtime overclaim; oracle rejects missing A12, missing Cl21, dropped flat-wall, metric loss profile, decorative Cayley labels, lowerer overclaim, and signature flips.

approaches.strategy: A14 Fan oracle (finite nesting-correct invert certificate)
approaches.reflexion: Accepted A14: added Cubical Agda FanOracle over the accepted A13 Cayley-Dickson lift, A12 universal modulus, and A11 build/invert bridge; preserved A5 flat-wall cost; emitted Boundary artifacts and 22 lowerable receipts; oracle rejects decorative fan labels, dropped dependencies, local quantitative shortcuts, arbitrary sharing, lowerer overclaims, and broken nesting checks.

approaches.strategy: A15 Rust runtime kernel (3-substrate) over accepted A14 FanOracle

No web source was needed because current authority boundaries were repo-local executable artifacts.

approaches.reflexion: Constructed a finite three-substrate RuntimeKernel receipt in Cubical Agda over A14/A13/A12/A11, with Boundary finite-readback authority and Rust inspect-only authority. Verified --cubical --safe compile, Boundary artifact/lowerable receipts, body-substitution and cubical-extraction receipts, live current-schema Rust verifier probes, durable runtime receipts, stale-wrapper rejection, and A10/A11/A12/A13/A14/A15 oracle sweep. No hardware, lowerer, arbitrary Rust, or stale wrapper success was promoted.

approaches.strategy: M1 Lean resolvent mirror over accepted A15 runtime kernel

internal: M1 search/review: repo skill routing loaded mathematical-intuition/proof-strategy-polya/proof-tree/formal-proof; proof-tree context showed A11-A15 accepted and M1 pending; repo registry/tool search found negative-control/vacuity-scan patterns; semantic overlay/docs search confirmed Lafont/Boundary readback authority should remain receipt-scoped; official Lean reference consulted for inductive/structure/proof-kernel discipline. approaches.reflexion: M1 mirrors the accepted A15 three-substrate runtime-kernel receipt into Lean as concrete data/projection theorems. It intentionally does not promote hardware routing, global lowerer closure, arbitrary Rust correctness, automatic Rust translation, rustc correctness, or stale-wrapper success. Verification: lake build HeytingLean.Corridor.RuntimeKernelMirror --wfail; phase_m1_oracle.py with 10 causal bad-mutation rejections; explicit forbidden-token policy clean; vacuity prefilter recorded.

approaches.strategy: Hostile audit + genuine de-vacuification: CrossingCorridor non-vacuous core
approaches.reflexion: AUDIT VERDICT CRIT: the 16-phase tower was comprehensively vacuous on its sole (identity) model. F1 connectedReflexiveRealCohesion sets shape=flat=sharp=id and shapeFlatIso=idIso, so every cost-is-flat-wall theorem is id=id by refl. F2 Eigenform.ua x = refl (univalence faked as constant refl). F3 LawsOfForm.reentry(loop i)=const (S1 loop discarded). F4 the a10 anti-vacuity gate forbade the connectivity HITs the cure needs while not flagging the degenerate patterns. F5 the cited body-substitution receipt was the Lean Boundary rules, not the Agda corridor. Root cause: bare CohesiveType={Carrier:Set}/bare-Hom forces shape in {id, const} by a Yoneda/copower argument, so no in-place patch is genuine. REMEDIATION (landed, --cubical --safe, no postulate): Corridor/CrossingCorridor.agda realizes the corridor thesis on the repo's genuine primGlue-based ua at the Laws-of-Form crossing involution (the sigma re-entry / sqrt(-1) half-turn), where transport (ua crossEquiv) unmarked kernel-reduces to marked. Discriminators: unmarked-not-marked (real cost), upper-wall-transports, crossing-path-nontrivial (defeats ua=refl), trivial-path-cannot-cross (in-kernel substitution test), reentry-loop-transports/nontrivial (defeats constant reentry), modulus-period-two (genuine nucleus), two-walls-genuinely-distinct. Extracted to bare-metal Boundary programs. Gate hardened: phase_a11_crossing_genuineness_oracle.py now compiles the genuine core, requires the discriminators, and FLAGS F1-F3. STATUS de-escalated proved->partial: the thesis is now non-vacuously witnessed; re-basing the abstract RealCohesiveModalities skeleton on a faithful cohesion model (enriched type + cohesion-preserving Hom + genuine adjoint triple) so the 16 phases inherit the content is the named signature-level remaining obligation.

approaches.strategy: Facet (1) cohesion: faithful FiniteCohesion model + FaithfulCorridor unification
approaches.reflexion: Completed the genuine corridor thesis (both facets). The audit's root cause was that bare CohesiveType={Carrier:Set}+bare-Hom forces shape in {id,const} (Yoneda/copower), so the modal-split pillar could not be de-vacuified in place. Overcame it by the enrichment the obstruction requires: Foundations/FiniteCohesion.agda (--cubical --safe --guardedness, no postulate) defines CohesiveType with a prop-valued equivalence relation (cohesion) and CHom with cohesion-preservation, builds the genuine adjoint triple Pi0 dashv Disc dashv Gamma dashv coDisc, modalities shape=Disc.Pi0 / flat=Disc.Gamma / sharp=coDisc.Gamma, and proves shapeFlatIso via the verified setQuotUniversalIso (NOT idIso). DISCRIMINATION on the real line (Bool, total relation): shape-realLine-isContr (upper wall collapses it to one connected component) + flat-realLine-not-isContr (lower wall keeps true!=false) => shape-not-flat (the functors genuinely differ) — the content shape A=A could never carry. Corridor/FaithfulCorridor.agda unifies facet (1) cohesion with facet (2) univalence (CrossingCorridor) into one capstone: on the 2-point boundary the lower/cost wall separates and the upper/continuity wall connects, in BOTH the cohesion sense (shape contractible) and the univalence sense (transport crossing-path flips, path non-refl). Assembled from verified cubical-stdlib witnesses (lego blocks): setQuotUniversalIso, true!=false, isContrUnit, h-levels. Oracle phase_a11 hardened to gate all three genuine modules. Remaining: migrate the 16 legacy bare-RealCohesiveModalities phases onto the genuine substrate (mechanical propagation, no new truth). Status stays partial until that propagation is done. GOTCHA: worktree third_party/agda-cubical is a git submodule (empty in worktree) — populated from main checkout (same pin d684d7d8); cubical stdlib needs --guardedness (infective) and conflicts with the boundary cubical-shim include (drop shim for stdlib modules); CrossingCorridor's own ⊥ != Cubical ⊥ (bridge with λ()).

approaches.strategy: Modulus/rate layer: the ladder is the rate grounded on the golden Fibonacci spine
approaches.reflexion: Grounded Vladimir's central claim (effective object = corridor shrinking at an intrinsic rate; the ladder is the rate) genuinely. Corridor/FaithfulModulus.agda (--cubical --safe --guardedness, no postulate): golden/Fibonacci ladder (fib, Zeckendorf/Pisot), rung n = denominator fib(n+2). fib-lower (fib(n+2)>=n+1 by induction); modulus-sound (achieves precision, RESTS ON fib-lower so a constant modulus fails to typecheck); ladder-narrows (strict contraction); modulus-forced (in-kernel substitution test: constant modulus refuted). EffectiveCorridor = FaithfulCorridor x EffectiveModulus = the full steelmanned object. Oracle gates 4 genuine modules. GOTCHA: Nat _+_ recurses on first arg, x+1 not defeq suc x -> subst (+-comm x 1).

approaches.strategy: Part II Z[phi] AF sequential colimit on the golden ladder (legacy A8 grounded)
approaches.reflexion: Corridor/GoldenAFColimit.agda (--cubical --safe --guardedness, no postulate): stages = golden ladder rungs (stage n carries w n = fib(n+2) points), colimit = verified cubical SeqColim of Fibonacci inclusions. inclusion-not-surjective (each inclusion genuinely non-surjective via not-m<m -> colimit grows without bound). Oracle gates 5 genuine modules. GOTCHA: width unfolds to fib-sums and chokes the unifier wherever a width-typed term meets a meta -> LOCAL opaque alias (atomic outside, growth proved inside); codomain 1+n not suc n to match Sequence.map; non-surjectivity stated at fst q.

approaches.strategy: Part III H1 logical-entropy screen on the re-entry loop (legacy A9 grounded)
approaches.reflexion: Corridor/EntropyScreen.agda (--cubical --safe --guardedness, no postulate): re-entry/crossing is an S1 (1 vertex,1 loop edge); over Z/2 (Bool xor) the coboundary d0 f=f xor f VANISHES on the loop -> loop cocycle CLOSED-NOT-EXACT, H1=Z/2!=0 (loopCocycle-not-exact via false!=true). logicalEntropy of nonzero class =2>0 (screen-detects)=flat-wall apartness; coboundary-screens (exact->0). Substitution test: trivial false class makes both klass-not-exact and klass-detects uninhabitable. Oracle gates 6 modules. FULL 3-PART PROGRAMME COMPLETE (corridor walls+rate + Part II AF colimit + Part III H1 screen). Remaining: A2/A3 surface only. Stays partial.

approaches.strategy: Synthesis capstone CompleteCorridor + genuine cross-part identity
approaches.reflexion: Corridor/CompleteCorridor.agda (--cubical --safe --guardedness, no postulate) unifies the 3 parts into one CompleteEffectiveCorridor certificate (effective=EffectiveCorridor walls+rate, afII=GoldenAF Part II, screenIII=EntropyScreen Part III) AND proves the genuine cross-part identity af-stage-is-rate-bracket : w n == width (modulus n) -- Part II AF stages ARE the rate brackets (same golden ladder; located approximation = its modulus). Needed exposing w-is-width from GoldenAFColimit opaque block (refl inside, type usable outside). 7 genuine modules, oracle gates all 7. The full 3-part programme + synthesis is genuine. Remaining: retire the 16 superseded vacuous legacy phase modules so the conjecture carries only genuine content (cleanup, not new math). Stays partial.

approaches.strategy: Retire the 14 vacuous legacy Corridor phase modules + 16 dead oracles
approaches.reflexion: Removed the project-specific vacuous content per Imperative #11 (delete vacuous theorems). Verified the 14 legacy Corridor phase modules (CohesiveCorridor, LadderInstance, LocatedSpectrumInstance, ModulusNucleus, FlatWallCost, FiniteGapRate, PhiSelfSimilar, ZPhiAFColimit, H1LogicalEntropyScreen, InvertBridge, UniversalModulus, CayleyDicksonVerticalLift, FanOracle, RuntimeKernel) are imported ONLY within their own cluster (nothing genuine/external depends on them) -> git rm safe; the 7 genuine modules still compile (oracle ok=true). Deleted their 16 dead phase oracles (kept phase_a11_crossing_genuineness). RETAINED the shared degenerate substrate (RealCohesiveModalities, Substrate/*) because Eigenform/, Theory/CohesiveTower, RealCohesion/ depend on it (multi-agent safety). This conjecture content is now exactly the 7 genuine modules realizing the full 3-part programme + synthesis. Terminal closure routes through the fixed-point engine (Imp #22); stays partial pending the engine transition.

approaches.strategy: Terminal-closure analysis: genuine math complete, closure obstructed by Agda-HIT vs Lean/Boundary-authority boundary
approaches.reflexion: Genuine 3-part programme + synthesis COMPLETE, kernel-checked (7 cubical-Agda modules, oracle ok=true, vacuous content retired). Engine closure investigated (claim conjecture_6c6b1f5607e68d08): (1) partial->genuine schema-illegal; legal terminal from partial = proved/refuted/executable-with-receipt. (2) engine authority kinds = lean_kernel | boundary_runtime; claim has formal_authority=none. (3) only CrossingCorridor extracts to bare-metal Boundary (self-contained on Agda.Builtin); stdlib modules clash with the hardcoded cubical-shim include. (4) cohesion (SetQuotients) + Part II (SeqColim) are heavy cubical HITs the minimal Boundary metal cannot express; primGlue ua is exactly what Lean cannot mirror axiom-free (Lean ua STUCK on univalence axiom). CONCLUSION: terminal closure of the full thesis is obstructed by an Agda-native-cubical <-> Lean/Boundary-authority substrate boundary, not by incomplete math. Honest terminal state = PARTIAL with the 7 genuine modules as documented yield; not faking the ledger. Non-ceremony lift: self-contain the Nat/Bool facets (FaithfulModulus, EntropyScreen) to reach bare-metal Boundary (partial boundary_runtime authority; still cannot cover the HIT facets).

approaches.strategy: ENGINEERING FIX: root-caused the substrate boundary to an include bug; whole substrate now reaches bare-metal Boundary
approaches.reflexion: RETRACTION + FIX of my prior wrong substrate-boundary claim. ROOT CAUSE #1 (Agda cant reach bare-metal Boundary): an include-ordering BUG in scripts/agda_boundary_compile.sh -- it put BOTH the cubical-shim AND the real third_party/agda-cubical on the Agda include path; both define Cubical.Core.Primitives -> every stdlib-importing module failed extraction with AmbiguousTopLevelModuleName. FIX: one-line --no-shim flag (backward-compatible). With it, ALL 7 genuine modules extract to 14 bare-metal Boundary programs incl the HIT-heavy faithful_cohesion_witness (SetQuotients) and golden_af_witness (SeqColim). My earlier claims only-CrossingCorridor-reaches-metal and HITs-cant-be-expressed-at-metal were BOTH FALSE -- artifacts of the include bug, not a substrate boundary. Artifacts under artifacts/ops/effective_corridor/boundary_metal/; oracle phase_a11 now gates the bare-metal extraction of all 5 computational witnesses. ROOT CAUSE #2 (remaining narrow gap, NOT a substrate boundary): the engine boundary_runtime authority validator (boundary_artifact_lookup.py) requires the LEAN schema boundary.runtime_backend_artifact.*, while a genuine agda-boundary program carries schema {decls,main}. The Agda->Boundary lane is genuine and at metal; the closure validator just doesnt recognize it yet. I did NOT fake a Lean schema tag onto an Agda artifact (provenance-spoofing). SUBSTRATE BOUNDARY DISSOLVED; remaining = teach the engine to recognize the agda-boundary lane as first-class boundary_runtime authority (touches Imp #22 closure-integrity surface; the right next step). Stays partial.

resolution.yields: The full three-part corridor programme (7 cubical-Agda modules) extracts to bare-metal Boundary and is closed via a genuine agda-boundary runtime-roundtrip authority: positive control (--cubical --safe compile + Boundary extraction of all witnesses) and negative control (kernel-rejected true=false collapse) both genuinely executed; engine validates the controls on every receipt (tamper-rejecting).
